name: chardet
what_it_does: Python library for automatically detecting the character encoding of byte strings. Uses statistical analysis to determine encoding (UTF-8, Latin-1, Windows-1252, Shift-JIS, etc.) from byte content. Essential for processing files or web content of unknown encoding. Returns encoding name and confidence score (0.0-1.0).
use_cases: Detect encoding of uploaded files before decoding: chardet.detect(file_bytes) returns encoding and confidence
Process legacy CSV/text files from external systems with unknown or mixed encodings
Web scraping: detect encoding of HTTP response body when Content-Type header is missing or wrong
Convert byte streams to strings in data pipelines that receive files from diverse sources
Fallback encoding detection when HTTP charset header is absent for web content processing
not_for: High-performance encoding detection — chardet is slow on large files; use charset-normalizer for better performance and accuracy
Binary file format detection (PDF, images) — chardet detects text encoding, not file types; use python-magic for file type detection
Production APIs with latency requirements — chardet analysis of large bytes can take significant time
best_when: Processing text files of unknown encoding where you need to decode bytes to strings with fallback.
avoid_when: Performance is critical — use charset-normalizer which is faster and more accurate for most cases.

auth.notes: Pure Python library — no auth needed.

pricing.free_tier_limits: LGPL license — free for most use
pricing.notes: LGPL licensed. Free for commercial use in most scenarios.

performance.rate_limits: None — local library. Analysis speed depends on byte stream size.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Returns None for encoding if confidence is too low. No exceptions for ambiguous inputs — always returns a result dict.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Deterministic analysis — same input bytes always produce the same result.
known_agent_gotchas: chardet.detect() returns None for 'encoding' key when confidence is 0 or content is all ASCII — always check result['encoding'] is not None before decoding
Low confidence results (< 0.5) are unreliable — fallback to UTF-8 or Latin-1 when confidence is low
chardet is not thread-safe for the UniversalDetector class — create a new detector per thread for concurrent usage
charset-normalizer is installed with requests as a chardet alternative — if requests is installed, charset_normalizer.from_bytes() is available without chardet
chardet is slow on large files — pass only the first 10-50KB for performance: chardet.detect(file_bytes[:50000])
ASCII detection returns 'ascii' encoding — you can safely decode ASCII with UTF-8 since ASCII is a subset of UTF-8

security_score_components.security_notes: Text encoding library — no network access. Ensure detected encoding is validated before using for security-sensitive decoding.
